plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs to zero.
Trim semiconductor and income holdings.
Reduce broad index exposure to lower concentration.
Buy new quality names with strong scanner reads.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation.
Expect lower volatility and smaller gains.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of roughly 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk. Expect lower potential gains but also a more stable ride.

plan: Eliminate highly leveraged ETFs.
Trim semiconductor and income holdings to reduce concentration.
Reduce broad index to meet concentration limits.
Increase position in strong AI/Software performer.
Add a new, high-quality tech name.
This balanced approach seeks risk-first growth.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month by reducing leverage and concentration while adding to strong performers. It blends broad market exposure with quality tech. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate.

plan: Reduce income and broad index holdings.
Trim some semiconductor exposure.
Add to the leveraged semiconductor ETF.
Increase position in strong AI/Software performer.
Buy new, top-rated tech names.
This aggressive plan aims for stretched growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets roughly 4% per month by increasing leverage and concentrating in high-scoring tech setups, including leveraged ETFs. Expect significantly higher potential upside but also much larger potential drawdowns and leverage decay. Strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Eliminate all leveraged and high-beta tech ETFs.
Sell semiconductor holdings to reduce volatility.
Significantly increase position in income-focused ETF.
Reduce broad index exposure to build cash buffer.
Focus on generating steady monthly distributions.
This plan prioritizes income and low drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets roughly 1% per month by prioritizing income-generating ETFs and broad index exposure for stability. It cuts leverage and growth-oriented single names to near zero. Expect reliable monthly distributions but limited capital appreciation and potential NAV declines in selloffs.
